details: The scheme "Solar Water Heating System (Domestic Sector)" implemented by the Renewable Energy Department, Government of Haryana, aims to promote renewable energy usage and reduce dependence on conventional heating methods by providing financial subsidies to encourage the installation of solar water heating systems in the domestic sector. It also seeks to reduce electricity and fuel bills and promote environmentally sustainable solutions for households.
benefits: ₹10,000/- per 100 LPD (Maximum ₹30,000/-) for FPC system and ₹3,000/- per 100 LPD (Maximum ₹9,000/-) is being provided.
eligibility: Government employees of Haryana residing in Chandigarh.

online: For registration, click on “New User/Register Here” and fill in all the mandatory details i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number & password. Click on ‘Submit’.
Verify the OTP received in the provided Email and Mobile Number. Alternatively, click on the Verification Link to activate the profile.
Login to Apply for the Scheme:
The “Sign in here” option is available on the right side of the screen. Fill in the required credentials and click on the ‘Login’ button.
In the opened window, click on “Scheme/Services list”, and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
Now, select the scheme and click on “Apply for Service/Scheme”.
Fill in all the details in the online application form and click on "Submit".
Type SARAL and send to 9954699899 to track your application.
Type SARAL<space><Application ID/Ticket No.> and send to 9954699899 to track your application/ticket.

documents_required: Identity/Citizenship Proof (Any one of the following):
Resident Proof/Address Proof/Ownership Proof (Any one of the following):

faqs.question: What is the objective of the “Solar Water Heating System (Domestic Sector)” scheme?
faqs.answer: The objective of the “Solar Water Heating System (Domestic Sector)” scheme is to encourage the adoption of solar energy by providing financial assistance to households, thereby reducing dependency on conventional energy sources and promoting eco-friendly domestic practices.

faqs.question: Who is eligible to apply for the benefits under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Any individual who is a permanent resident of Haryana or is a Haryana Government employee residing in Chandigarh is considered eligible to apply for the subsidy provided under the “Solar Water Heating System (Domestic Sector)” scheme.

faqs.question: What kind of financial assistance is provided through this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme provides a financial subsidy of ₹10,000/- per 100 litres per day (LPD) for Flat Plate Collector (FPC) systems, subject to a maximum limit of ₹30,000/-, and ₹3,000/- per 100 LPD for other systems, with a maximum cap of ₹9,000/-.

faqs.question: Can residents from outside Haryana apply for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, only individuals who are either permanent residents of Haryana or Haryana Government employees residing in Chandigarh are permitted to apply and benefit under this scheme.

faqs.question: Is the scheme available for both rural and urban residents?
faqs.answer: Yes, the “Solar Water Heating System (Domestic Sector)” scheme is applicable to all eligible residents across both rural and urban areas within the geographical eligibility limits defined by the scheme.

faqs.question: Is there any income limit or caste-based reservation for this scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme does not impose any income ceiling or provide caste-based reservations, and all Haryana residents who meet the technical and procedural criteria are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: What is the process for applying to the "Solar Water Heating System (Domestic Sector)"?
faqs.answer: The applicants may visit the Antyodaya-SARAL Portal to apply for the scheme.

faqs.question: What is the importance of the commissioning report in the application process?
faqs.answer: The commissioning report is a mandatory document that confirms the solar water heating system has been installed correctly and is functional; it serves as official evidence required for the release of the subsidy.

faqs.question: What is the offline process for applying the scheme?
faqs.answer: To apply for the "Scheme of Solar Inverter Charger", the applicant may visit the nearest Atal Seva Kendra (Common Service Centre).

faqs.question: Is there any fee applicable for applying this scheme through Atal Seva Kendra (Common Service Centre)?
faqs.answer: Yes, a nominal service fee of ₹10/- is charged by the Common Service Centre for the application.
